The $1,232 intraday high is next resistance; clearing it opens a test of $1,238 (June 29 high) and then new ATH territory. DCF $2,022 vs $1,213 = 40% M

news_catalyst.headline: No specific LLY news — Wyckoff Phase E markup continues post-LPS
news_catalyst.summary: LLY has no breaking news today — which is characteristic of Wyckoff Phase E markup: orderly advance on moderate volume driven by the absence of sellers. The June 26 SOS (2.35x volume, $128 range) established the new base; the July 1 LPS ($1,173 low, 0.77x) was the final shakeout; today's recovery to $1,213 on 0.97x volume is the early markup phase. The $1,180 LPS low held for the second consecutive session. GLP-1 fundamentals (Zepbound, Mounjaro) remain structurally intact.

technical.summary: LLY is in the most advanced Wyckoff phase in the watchlist: Phase E (Complete). Today's 0.97x near-average volume on a +1.86% session is textbook markup — price rising as supply is absent. The $1,180.30 LPS held, confirming it as the support floor. The $1,232 intraday high is next resistance; clearing it opens a test of $1,238 (June 29 high) and then new ATH territory. DCF $2,022 vs $1,213 = 40% MoS makes this the strongest fundamentals/technicals combination in the watchlist.

wyckoff_narrative: Phase E is Wyckoff's endgame — accumulation complete, markup underway. The SOS on June 26 (2.35x) was the breakout event. The LPS on July 1 ($1,173, 0.77x) was the final shakeout. Today's recovery to $1,213 on drying volume is the early markup. Expected next event: continued advance past $1,238 prior high. Any pullback to $1,173-1,180 on drying volume is a buyable LPS retest. Close below $1,128 (SOS open) on volume would invalidate.

valuation.summary: DCF $2,022 vs $1,213 = 40% MoS. GLP-1 obesity/diabetes market represents a decades-long growth runway. Bull case DCF significantly higher given TAM uncertainty. Margin of safety is real and substantial.

risks: Trump drug pricing executive orders — binary risk, can cause 5-10% single-day selloffs
Position sizing: $1,213/share = $121K per 100 shares; plan lot sizes carefully
